user_message: Help me build migration architecture for Iron Creek Mining.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 10:36 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Iron Creek Mining

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-13 13:32 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining migration architecture

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 09:58 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-02-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Michelle (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:01:30] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into migration architecture.

[00:05:00] Michelle (Iron Creek Mining): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:10:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Michelle (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:17:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Michelle (Iron Creek Mining): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
